_inspectorSnowReport.Report: Saturday, March 7, 5:25 a.m. Welcome to the weekend! Two storms hit us this week, delivering 29 inches of new snow to the mountain. We received 6 inches of new snow during the day yesterday before the skies cleared last night. Today should be an excellent day for skiing and riding with packed powder groomers on the front side and plenty of untouched fluff through terrain gates. Patrol opened the west face of Honeycomb Canyon yesterday and will be working to open the east face today. Temperatures have remained cold since the last storm, so expect to find some nice powder back there! Respect all rope lines and closures as avalanche mitigation work takes place this morning. The skies will be sunny today, perfect for exploring the mountain and enjoying the live DJ on the Moonbeam patio. The fun continues after the sun goes down with Nordic Nights at the Solitude Nordic & Snowshoe Center. Join us for the Longest Day on Sunday — set your clocks forward and ride Link, Apex Express, and Moonbeam Express from 8:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.
